meta.note: 80-150 word answers in Jamie's voice. Adapt before submitting.

answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
answers.answer: This role sits right at the intersection of my two favorite things—leadership development and the stakeholder coordination that makes it real for students. In my current role I coordinate 20+ cohort-based educational programs, managing the logistics, vendors, and continuous improvement that keep each cohort running smoothly. What excites me about the Program Coordinator position is doing that work in service of MBA students who are actively practicing how to lead. I love the operational side—the calendars, the details, the small course corrections—but I love it most when it's in service of a program that genuinely changes how people show up. That's the kind of impact I'd be proud to help deliver at Johnson.

answers.question: Why Cornell / Johnson?
answers.answer: I'm drawn to Johnson because you treat leadership development as something to be designed and continuously improved, not just delivered—building experiential learning and reflection into the program so students actually practice leading and get meaningful feedback. My graduate training in Applied Organizational Psychology is grounded in that same systems mindset: diagnose, design, measure, refine. I also value that Johnson develops principled, self-aware leaders with the character and courage to do good in the world; as someone who cares deeply about people and growth, that mission resonates. I'd love to help deliver a leadership experience that has a profoundly positive impact on students, and to do it within a community that takes both the craft and the purpose of it seriously.

answers.question: Describe your experience coordinating cohort or leadership-development programs.
answers.answer: As a Program Enablement Manager, I coordinate the planning, delivery, and continuous improvement of 20+ cohort-based educational programs—managing logistics across 10+ vendors and 70+ cross-functional staff, and running training webinars for 600+ participants at a 78% enrollment rate. Earlier, at Vestas, I piloted an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders that was later scaled to 23 global locations; that taught me how much a leadership-development experience depends on the operations behind it. I approach this work the way I was trained as an organizational psychologist: I conduct needs assessments with stakeholders to find the real gaps, design around them, and build feedback loops so each cohort runs better than the last.
